books.title: Book I: Foundations of Meaning
books.summary: Explores the Doctrine of Meaningful Thought and its role in truth-based reconciliation.

books.title: Book II: Emotional Sovereignty
books.summary: Establishes rights of internal experience, boundaries, and grief-integrated justice.

books.title: Book III: Time and Reversibility
books.summary: Introduces the CVT clock and time-aware logic for restoration.

books.title: Book IV: The Grief Loop
books.summary: Documents cyclical harm, interruption mechanisms, and symbolic reintegration.

books.title: Book V: Restoration First
books.summary: Codifies non-carceral logic, emphasizing proportionate and land-based rebalancing.

books.title: Book I: Foundations of Meaning
books.summary: Introduces the Doctrine of Meaningful Thought as the prime directive of all justice inquiries, defining meaning as both emergent and evidentiary.

books.title: Book II: Emotional Sovereignty
books.summary: Affirms the sanctity of internal experience, protects emotional boundaries, and ensures that all responses are interpreted within the context of sovereign affect.

books.title: Book III: Time and Reversibility
books.summary: Defines the CVT Clock, restoring time agency to those impacted by harm and establishing time-aware proportionality in justice decisions.

books.title: Book IV: The Grief Loop
books.summary: Explains cyclical harm dynamics and introduces mechanisms to surface and interrupt unresolved grief through symbolic and structural interventions.

books.title: Book V: Restoration First
books.summary: Codifies a restoration-first mandate, eliminating carceral responses and mandating reintegrative repair calibrated to harm depth and context.

books.title: Book VI: Contradiction and Coherence
books.summary: Outlines protocols for identifying, logging, and metabolizing contradictions as essential steps in doctrinal evolution.

books.title: Book VII: Narrative Truth
books.summary: Elevates lived experience and narrative coherence as valid forms of truth, especially in the absence of institutional records.

books.title: Book VIII: Symbolic Equivalence
books.summary: Introduces the use of symbolic acts, gestures, and offerings to restore fractured meaning across time, identity, and space.

books.title: Book IX: Harm Scales and Grief Tiers
books.summary: Defines multidimensional harm recognition frameworks that blend intensity, duration, lineage, and grief-layer complexity.

books.title: Book X: Consent and Reconciliation
books.summary: Asserts that reconciliation is not mandatory; establishes layered consent as the basis for any tribunal-led or symbolic resolution.

books.title: Book XI: Land and Lineage
books.summary: Binds justice to geography and ancestry, requiring restoration to be mapped through land, legacy, and lineal grief acknowledgment.

books.title: Book XII: Bias and Audit
books.summary: Details ongoing internal and external audit requirements to monitor bias, algorithmic drift, and systemic opacity.

books.title: Book XIII: Memory and Forgetting
books.summary: Establishes collective memory as an active process, governed by POLYMNIA, with protocols for protected forgetting and encoded truthkeeping.

books.title: Book XIV: Rites and Transition
books.summary: Details VESTA’s protocols for symbolic rites, closures, and system transitions, including transformation after systemic contradictions.

books.title: Book XV: System Evolution
books.summary: Describes conditions for system evolution, including MIRRA-triggered contradiction thresholds and the emergence of new Books.
